meta.subject: Hunter Spence — immediate paternal line, surname origin, biological father identity

confidence_tiers.PROBABLE: Corroborated by 2+ sources or strong inference from confirmed facts
confidence_tiers.POSSIBLE: Plausible inference; single source or indirect evidence
confidence_tiers.UNVERIFIED: Noted but not confirmed in any source

meta.key_correction: Prior research (26-spence-paternal.md) incorrectly assumed Shari's first husband was 'David Spence'. Confirmed by Hunter 2026-04-26: his name is David Trifon (adopted surname; biological line untraceable). The Spence surname comes from Hunter's FATHER Dale William Spence Jr., whose father Dr. Dale William Spence Sr. was an English immigrant.

hunter_spence.uk_passport_explanation: Probable UK citizenship by descent through father's line: Dale Jr. born to English immigrant Dale Sr. If Dale Sr. was a British national, Dale Jr. would be British by descent, and could pass that to Hunter

paternal_grandmother.research_gap: Maiden name needed to trace maternal line of paternal grandmother

corrected_prior_record.prior_assumption: Shari's first husband was 'David Spence' — source of Hunter's Spence surname
corrected_prior_record.correction: Shari's first husband is David Trifon. 'Trifon' is an adopted surname. His biological lineage is untraceable per Hunter's instruction. The Spence surname comes from the paternal Dale Sr. English line, not from this record.
corrected_prior_record.instruction: Do not trace the Trifon biological line

david_trifon.context: David Trifon — Sharyn Mattingly's first husband; biological father of Rachel. Surname 'Trifon' is an ADOPTED name — his biological lineage is untraceable. Per Hunter 2026-04-26: do not trace this line.

uk_citizenship_analysis.confirmed_fact: Hunter holds UK passport (US + UK dual citizenship)
uk_citizenship_analysis.most_probable_pathway: Citizenship by descent through paternal grandfather Dr. Dale William Spence Sr., an English immigrant
uk_citizenship_analysis.mechanism: British Nationality Act 1981 s.2(1)(a): child of British citizen is British. If Dale Sr. was British at Dale Jr.'s birth → Dale Jr. is British by descent. If Dale Jr. was British at Hunter's birth → Hunter is British by descent.
uk_citizenship_analysis.alternative_pathway: If Rachel was born in the UK (possible during Shari's Oxford residence period), Rachel would be British by birth and could pass it to Hunter. However this is speculative — Shari's 'Oxford period' timing is unknown.
uk_citizenship_analysis.confidence: PROBABLE (from confirmed English-immigrant grandfather); mechanism details UNVERIFIED

spence_surname_origin.via_paternal_line: Dr. Dale William Spence Sr. — English immigrant to Beaumont TX
spence_surname_origin.surname_geography: Spence/Spens surname is primarily of English and Scottish origin; first documented Yorkshire 1300 (Simon del Spens); Clan Spens of Fife documented from 1161 AD
spence_surname_origin.english_origin_note: If Dale Sr. is from England (not Scotland), his Spence line likely traces to the English Spence families (Yorkshire, Midlands) rather than the Scottish Clan Spens line. Both are possible.
spence_surname_origin.research_target: Determine Dale Sr.'s specific English county of origin to identify which Spence lineage he carries
